content:
I tried to add a config which indicates if Rust/clang LLVM are compatible (currently just major version check), but I think I can't run a shell command based on other CONFIG variables?

i.e. I tried this and it doesn't work:

diff --git a/init/Kconfig b/init/Kconfig
index c6b744a48dcca..30bfbc70e179c 100644
--- a/init/Kconfig
+++ b/init/Kconfig
@@ -78,6 +78,20 @@ config RUST_IS_AVAILABLE
          In particular, the Makefile target 'rustavailable' is useful to check
          why the Rust toolchain is not being detected.

+config RUSTC_LLVM_VERSION
+       int
+       default $(shell,$(srctree)/scripts/rustc-llvm-version.sh $(RUSTC))
+       help
+         It does not depend on `RUST` since that one may need to use the version
+         in a `depends on`.
+
+config RUSTC_CLANG_LLVM_COMPAT
+       def_bool $(shell,expr $(CONFIG_RUSTC_LLVM_VERSION) / 10000 = $(CONFIG_CLANG_VERSION) / 10000)
+       help
+         This determines if Rust and Clang versions are compatible.
+
+         Currently this checks that the major versions that LLVM match.
+

Do I have to write a script that calls to both rustc and clang directly to determine LLVM compatibility and return the result?
@Alice Ryhl I think you want to have something similar to this?
I already have the rustc-llvm-version.sh script written locally
If you want to make the follow-up I mentioned in this patch, then please go ahead.

Do I have to write a script that calls to both rustc and clang directly to determine LLVM compatibility and return the result?

Probably -- I have not seen an evaluation of a CONFIG_ symbol inside Kconfig before and I can't find one grepping a bit.
Alternatively I was thinking of modifying existing scripts to have a --major option, and add RUSTC_LLVM_MAJOR_VERSION and CLANG_MAJOR_VERSION
Would it make sense to move rustc (and its associated LLVM) version check to scripts/Kconfig.include? This way I can reference one variable from another
